metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify concretely how `e6_judge_reliability.py` obtains a `{PASS, BLOCK, INDETERMINATE}` verdict for each permutation — either by calling the production `rebar.llm.plan_review.__init__.review_plan(ticket_id)` (which handles PlanContext internally) or by documenting the harness-level verdict derivation that approximates `finalize_verdict`.
evidence: orchestrator.finalize_verdict signature (lines 467-475): `(ctx: PlanContext, parts: dict[str, list[dict]], *, coaching, coverage, runner_name, model)` — requires PlanContext and partitioned parts
three_pass.run_three_pass() (line 338-369) returns a flat list of per-finding dicts with 'decision' field — no PASS/BLOCK/INDETERMINATE verdict, no PlanContext
The harness has no PlanContext import or instantiation path
workflow_ops.py:189 shows finalize_verdict is called after a full orchestration pipeline, not standalone
findings.finding: Experiment B's 'full gate verdict path' claim is not achievable via `orchestrator.finalize_verdict` alone from a standalone harness. `finalize_verdict` requires a `PlanContext` dataclass and a pre-partitioned `parts` dict (`{blocking, surfaced, overflow, indeterminate, dropped}`) that are produced deep inside the production workflow. The harness `three_pass.run_three_pass()` returns a flat list of per-finding decision dicts — not the `parts` shape `finalize_verdict` expects.
findings.location: Experiment B — Mechanism section: 'run the full gate verdict path (Pass-1 → Pass-2 → Pass-3 → finalize_verdict)'
scenarios: The e6_judge_reliability.py harness author attempts to call orchestrator.finalize_verdict and must either import the full production dependency chain (breaking the 'no src/rebar changes' boundary) or replicate the parts-assembly logic

The harness derives 'verdict' from the list of decisions using its own logic that differs from finalize_verdict's DET-block / llm_unavailable / verify_failed branches — creating a different verdict space than what the plan claims to measure

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `repo_root` to the frozen input schema for `e6_selfconsistency_inputs.jsonl` (or document the fallback policy) and specify how the harness supplies a valid `repo_root` at judge-run time so agentic mode reliably activates for G6/E4/T3 findings.
evidence: three_pass.py:185: `grounded = agentic and repo_root and any(c in CODEBASE_GROUNDED for c in finding.get('criteria', []))`
three_pass.py:187: `if grounded: return _verify_agentic(...)` — requires repo_root truthy
The plan's frozen input schema: `{finding_id, ticket_id, title, plan, criterion, finding, location, suggested_fix}` — no repo_root field listed
The corpus findings are from a live store; their original repo_roots (e.g., `/Users/joeoakhart/rebar`) may not exist on the machine running E6
findings.finding: The plan does not specify how `repo_root` is supplied to `three_pass.pass2_verify(agentic=True)` during Exp A re-judging. The agentic path only activates when `repo_root` is non-empty AND the criterion is in `CODEBASE_GROUNDED`. The frozen input file `e6_selfconsistency_inputs.jsonl` stores `{finding_id, ticket_id, title, plan, criterion, finding, location, suggested_fix}` — no `repo_root` field. Without a valid repo_root at judge-run time, `pass2_verify` silently falls back to single-turn mode even when `agentic=True` is passed.
findings.location: Experiment A — Re-judge inputs are frozen section and Judge run section: 'three_pass.pass2_verify(title, plan, finding, repo_root, agentic=True)'
scenarios: E6 runs on a CI machine where /Users/joeoakhart/rebar does not exist; all G6/E4/T3 findings fall back to single-turn mode; E6 measures single-turn reliability instead of agentic reliability, invalidating the R5 gating logic
repo_root is supplied as the current checkout but the agentic verifier searches for symbols named in findings from archived tickets — findings about code that may have changed or been deleted
findings.suggested_fix: Add `"repo_root": "/path/to/rebar"` to each frozen input row and document that E6 must be run from a checkout of the rebar repo; alternatively, pin a specific git SHA and use the snapshot read path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **T8 / Exp A & B fallback protocol:** Specify how `e6_judge_reliability.py` distinguishes infra failures from judge-produced INDETERMINATE — e.g., check `mode.startswith('error:')` or `mode == 'agentic-no-verdict'` in Exp A, and add an `infra_failure` flag to Exp B output rows — so infra-INDETERMINATE exclusion/re-run logic is implementable.
evidence: Plan references `llm_unavailable` as the detection signal for infra-INDETERMINATE.
`llm_unavailable` only appears in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py:491` (the production gate's `coverage` dict), not in any harness path.

In `three_pass.py:197-198`, API failure returns `{verify: None, mode: 'error:...'}`; `pass3_decide(None)` at line 291-293 returns `decision='indeterminate', reason='no-verification'` — no `llm_unavailable` flag.
ABSENCE: The plan never specifies how `e6_judge_reliability.py` will detect and exclude infra failures vs judge-produced INDETERMINATE verdicts in the harness context.
findings.finding: The plan's exclusion rule for infra-INDETERMINATE verdicts in Exp B ('judge-produced infra-INDETERMINATE excluded, re-run') and Exp A ('no-verification runs excluded') references a detection signal (`llm_unavailable`) that exists only in `orchestrator.finalize_verdict`'s `coverage` dict — a path not used by the E6 harness. In the harness, API failures on `pass2_verify` return `{verify: None, mode: 'error:...'}`, and `pass3_decide(None)` emits `decision='indeterminate', reason='no-verification'` — structurally identical to a judge-produced INDETERMINATE. No field distinguishes infra failure from judge abstention in this code path.
findings.location: Grounded definitions section: 'Infra-INDETERMINATE (LLM tier unavailable, `llm_unavailable`) is not judge instability — such runs are excluded and re-run'; Experiment A judge run section
scenarios: An API rate-limit during an E6 Exp A run causes `pass2_verify` to return `{verify: None, mode: 'error:429'}` for finding #23; this appears as `decision='indeterminate'` and is counted as a disagreement vote, artificially deflating κ and potentially producing a false R5 blocker.

An Exp B full-gate run returns INDETERMINATE because a model tier was temporarily unavailable; without a detection mechanism, this permutation is counted as a valid judge-produced INDETERMINATE and skewed into the agreement metric.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the E6 driver spec: 'Infra failure detection in the harness: for Exp A, a vote is infra-failed if the raw `pass2_verify` result has `mode` starting with `error:` or equals `agentic-no-verdict`; for Exp B, a permutation run is infra-failed if `finalize_verdict` raises or returns a coverage dict with `llm_unavailable=True`. Infra-failed votes/runs are logged separately, excluded from κ computation, and retried up to 2× before being flagged as unresolvable.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In the Exp B Mechanism section, specify the wiring from `run_three_pass` output to `finalize_verdict` inputs: how `partition_findings` is called, how a stub `PlanContext` and empty `coaching`/`coverage` are assembled, or alternatively confirm that `run_three_pass`'s per-finding `decision` values are sufficient and replace the `finalize_verdict` reference with the actual aggregation logic the harness will use.
evidence: `three_pass.run_three_pass()` returns a list of per-finding dicts with `decision`, `confidence`, `severity` etc. — no aggregate `verdict` field and no `parts` partition (three_pass.py:338-369)

`orchestrator.finalize_verdict` signature at `orchestrator.py:467-475` requires `ctx: PlanContext`, `parts: dict[str, list[dict]]`, `coaching: list[dict]`, `coverage: dict`, `runner_name`, `model` — none of which `run_three_pass` returns directly
No existing harness (`final_gate_3pass.py`, `run_review_foundation.py`, etc.) calls `finalize_verdict` — they stop at `run_three_pass` decisions
The plan's AC for Exp B asserts `verdict ∈ {PASS,BLOCK,INDETERMINATE}` per plan×permutation, which requires correctly invoking `finalize_verdict` — a non-trivial integration the plan does not specify
findings.finding: Exp B specifies running the 'full gate verdict path (Pass-1 → Pass-2 → Pass-3 → `finalize_verdict`)' to obtain `verdict ∈ {PASS,BLOCK,INDETERMINATE}`, but the existing harness entry point `three_pass.run_three_pass()` returns per-finding decision records — not the aggregate verdict — and `orchestrator.finalize_verdict` requires a `PlanContext`, a `parts` partition dict (`blocking`, `surfaced`, `overflow`, `indeterminate`, `dropped`), `coaching`, `coverage`, `runner_name`, and `model` arguments that no existing harness assembles. The plan provides no wiring spec for how `e6_judge_reliability.py` will bridge `run_three_pass` output into `finalize_verdict` inputs.
findings.location: Experiment B — Mechanism section: 'run the full gate verdict path (Pass-1 → Pass-2 → Pass-3 → `finalize_verdict`)'; Scope section: '`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/e6_judge_reliability.py`'
scenarios: Author implements `e6_judge_reliability.py` and discovers `finalize_verdict` cannot be called without also importing `partition_findings`, constructing a `PlanContext`, and running a coaching pass — requiring substantial undocumented scaffolding

Author substitutes a simpler proxy (e.g. checking whether any finding has `decision == 'block'`) that does not faithfully replicate `finalize_verdict`'s logic, producing results incompatible with the plan's stated metric

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that Exp A targets the harness's `three_pass.pass2_verify` (not the production `review_kernel.verify_findings`) and update the gating rationale for R5 accordingly, OR redirect Exp A to invoke the production verifier path.
evidence: src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:534-535: 'the bespoke pass2_verify was retired in epic solid-timer-unison WS1'

src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py:279 references 'matching bespoke run_review's pass2_verify(agentic=grounded); when false the cheaper' — indicating the bespoke path is now a compatibility shim or gone
The production gate uses `rebar.llm.review_kernel.verify_findings` via the engine workflow, not the harness `three_pass.pass2_verify`
The plan says E6 'measures the existing gate' but `three_pass.pass2_verify` is in the experiment harness dir, not src/rebar
findings.finding: The production Pass-2 verifier has been retired and replaced: `three_pass.pass2_verify` is a harness-only function, not the current production verifier. The production gate now routes through `rebar.llm.review_kernel.verify_findings` (via the engine workflow). Exp A measures the harness verifier's reliability, but the plan frames it as measuring 'the existing gate's' judge stability — this is a mislabeled scope.
findings.location: Experiment A — Judge run section: 'three_pass.pass2_verify(title, plan, finding, repo_root, agentic=True)' and 'the agentic Pass-2 verifier ... the R5 cohort's plan_review_verifier_agentic path'
scenarios: E6 passes (κ≥0.6) but the result is about the harness verifier, not the production verifier R5 actually depends on; R5 proceeds on a reliability guarantee that doesn't apply to the code it will modify
E6 fails but the production verifier (review_kernel.verify_findings) is actually stable — the block is a false gate

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a cardinality assertion to the AC3 proving command verifying that the output row count matches the sum of input permutations (e.g., `assert len(o) == sum(c.values())` after building the Counter from inputs).
evidence: The output assertion only checks `all(r['verdict'] in {'PASS','BLOCK','INDETERMINATE'} for r in o)` — a single valid row satisfies this
No cross-join between input plan_ids/permutation_ids and output rows
Cardinality defect type 5 from the VERIFY-COMMAND DEFECT TAXONOMY: 'missing cardinality assertion when the criterion names a count'
findings.finding: The proving command for AC3 (Exp B run recorded) validates that each input plan_id has ≥3 permutation entries but does NOT assert that the output file `e6_ordershuffle.jsonl` has a corresponding verdict row for every plan×permutation — a partial run (e.g., 15 plans × 1 verdict each) would pass the command.
findings.location: AC: 'Exp B run recorded' — proving command `python3 -c "... assert all(r['verdict'] in {'PASS','BLOCK','INDETERMINATE'} for r in o) ..."`
scenarios: A harness bug that writes only the first permutation's verdict per plan (15 rows instead of 45) would pass the proving command while leaving the experiment incomplete.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the proving command: `oc=collections.Counter((r['plan_id'],r['permutation_id']) for r in o); assert set(oc.keys())=={(r['plan_id'],r['permutation_id']) for r in ins}, 'missing output rows'; print('OK', len(oc), 'plan×permutation verdicts recorded')`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **T8 / Exp B Mechanism:** Specify the `permute_sections` selection rule — e.g., 'sample 3 distinct non-identity permutations with `random.seed(<value>)` from the uniform distribution over N! orderings, or take the first 3 of `itertools.permutations(sections)` excluding the identity' — and state the seed or enumeration strategy so the build step is reproducible.
evidence: Plan states: 'generate 3 section-order permutations by reordering its top-level `##` section blocks (content preserved verbatim; a pure helper `permute_sections`)'
No seed value, enumeration strategy, or selection algorithm is stated for `permute_sections`.
Frozen permutation specs are committed to `runs/e6_ordershuffle_inputs.jsonl` — but the BUILD step that creates this file has no specified algorithm, so a re-run from scratch would be irreproducible.
ABSENCE: The plan has no analogue to the `SEED = 1729` pattern used in `build_adjudication_corpus.py`.
findings.finding: Exp B specifies '3 section-order permutations' per plan but never defines how those 3 are selected from the N! possible orderings. The helper `permute_sections` is referenced but its selection protocol (random with seed, exhaustive up to 3, sorted enumeration, etc.) is unspecified — this is a decision rule co-located with the schema that is missing.
findings.location: Experiment B — Mechanism section: 'generate 3 section-order permutations by reordering its top-level `##` section blocks'
scenarios: Two executors implement `permute_sections` with different seeds or strategies; the committed `e6_ordershuffle_inputs.jsonl` is generated by one, but a reviewer trying to reproduce from scratch gets different permutations.
A plan with exactly 3 sections has only 3! = 6 permutations — does the helper use all 3 non-identity permutations, or 3 randomly selected (possibly including the identity)?
findings.suggested_fix: Add: 'For plans with ≥3 sections, take permutations [1], [2], [3] of `list(itertools.permutations(sections))[1:4]` (lexicographic order, skipping the identity at index 0); for 2-section plans, use the single non-identity permutation three times (or exclude from the denominator). Fix `random.seed(1729)` if random selection is preferred.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **T8 / Exp A sample frame:** State the explicit seed value for the N=50 deterministic sample (e.g., `SEED = 1729` matching project convention) so the build step that generates `runs/e6_selfconsistency_inputs.jsonl` is independently reproducible.
evidence: Plan says 'fixed seed' but supplies no seed value.
`build_adjudication_corpus.py:60` establishes `SEED = 1729` as the project convention for deterministic sampling from this same corpus.
ABSENCE: No seed value appears anywhere in the E6 plan text.
The committed `runs/e6_selfconsistency_inputs.jsonl` is the frozen output — but the BUILD step that generates it is the harness code, which must specify a seed to be reproducible.
findings.finding: Exp A states findings are 'sampled deterministically (fixed seed)' but never states the seed value, making the N=50 cohort non-reproducible by a different executor. The existing project convention (SEED=1729 in `build_adjudication_corpus.py`) is not referenced or adopted.
findings.location: Experiment A — Sample frame: 'N = 50 findings, sampled deterministically (fixed seed) from the 74-member cohort'
scenarios: A reviewer trying to reproduce E6 from scratch picks seed=42, selects a different N=50 sample, and gets different κ results — invalidating the recorded gate verdict for R5.
The seed is left to the implementer; two team members produce conflicting inputs files.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Exp A sample frame: 'Sampled with `random.seed(1729)` (matching the project's `build_adjudication_corpus.py` convention) after deterministic sort by `finding_id`; the seed is recorded in the harness file header comment.'

evidence: vague/subjective terms present: robust
findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **T8 / AC 4:** Declare the `fleiss_kappa` implementation source — either name the library+import (`statsmodels.stats.inter_rater.fleiss_kappa`) or state it will be hand-rolled with an inline formula reference — so the unit-test fixture's expected κ value is unambiguous.
evidence: Plan states: 'tests/unit/test_e6_agreement.py asserts `fleiss_kappa` and `raw_agreement` on hand-computed fixtures (incl. a perfect-agreement fixture → κ==1.0, raw==1.0, and a known-κ table)'
The plan never names a library, import path, or formula for `fleiss_kappa`.
ABSENCE: No `import statsmodels` or equivalent appears anywhere in the harnesses directory; no formula is stated in the plan.

A schema/enum-adjacent gap: the function's *computational contract* (which formula for Fleiss' κ) is undefined, meaning the 'known-κ table' fixture value cannot be computed without picking an implementation.
findings.finding: `fleiss_kappa` is designated a 'pure helper' to be unit-tested on hand-computed fixtures including 'a known-κ table', but the plan never specifies whether it will be hand-rolled or imported from a library (e.g., `statsmodels.stats.inter_rater`). An executor must choose an implementation without guidance, risking a mismatch between the unit-test fixture's expected value and the chosen algorithm's output.
findings.location: Scope section (`tests/unit/test_e6_agreement.py` entry) and AC 4 (`fleiss_kappa` on hand-computed fixtures)
scenarios: Executor hand-rolls κ using the standard formula but gets a boundary case wrong; unit test passes on the wrong value because the fixture was generated by the same hand-rolled code.
Executor imports statsmodels but it's not in the project's declared dependencies; CI fails on import; the fix changes the κ value slightly, invalidating the committed fixture.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope or AC 4: 'Implemented as a hand-rolled function using the standard Fleiss (1971) formula: p̄_e = Σ_j p_j², κ = (P̄_o − P̄_e) / (1 − P̄_e); or via `statsmodels.stats.inter_rater.fleiss_kappa` (declare as a dev dependency). The unit-test fixture must be generated from the same formula.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the write idempotency contract for all output files: on re-run, the harness must detect and skip already-written records (keyed by finding_id/plan_id+permutation), or overwrite atomically (write to temp then rename), to prevent duplicate rows.
evidence: AC proof: 'per={i:0 for i in ids}; [per.__setitem__(r["finding_id"], per[r["finding_id"]]+1) for r in v]; assert all(c==3 for c in per.values())' — fails if any finding_id has 6 votes due to append-on-rerun
ABSENCE: no mention of overwrite vs append behavior, no idempotency guard on output writes
T9 UPDATE lifecycle: 'who updates and under what conditions' is unspecified for committed run artifacts
findings.finding: The plan creates multiple output JSONL/CSV/JSON files but does not specify what happens on a partial run and re-run: whether the harness overwrites, appends, or skips already-written records. Appending on re-run would create duplicate rows, breaking the AC proof scripts (which assert exact vote counts per finding_id).
findings.location: Scope section (output files); AC 'Exp A run recorded' proving script; AC 'Exp B run recorded' proving script
scenarios: Harness crashes after writing 30 findings; operator re-runs; output JSONL now has 30 duplicated + 50 new entries; AC assertion c==3 fails for the duplicated 30
findings.suggested_fix: At harness startup, load any existing output file into a seen-set of (finding_id, vote_index) or (plan_id, permutation_index). Skip records already in the seen-set. Alternatively, write all outputs to a temp file and rename atomically at completion, accepting that a mid-run crash requires a full re-run from scratch (simpler correctness guarantee).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the exact permutation-selection algorithm in Exp B's Mechanism section (e.g., 'pick permutation indices [0, N//3, 2*N//3] of the sorted section list with fixed seed 42').
evidence: 'generate 3 section-order permutations by reordering its top-level ## section blocks'
'Frozen permutation specs are committed to runs/e6_ordershuffle_inputs.jsonl'
No algorithm (random seed, lexicographic order, etc.) specified for selecting which 3 of the N! possible permutations to use
findings.finding: The plan commits to generating exactly 3 section-order permutations per plan for Exp B but does not specify HOW those 3 permutations are selected from the combinatorial space (e.g., random with fixed seed, first N lexicographic, manually curated). Since `runs/e6_ordershuffle_inputs.jsonl` is 'frozen and committed' via a build step, the executor must know the generation algorithm to produce the committed file reproducibly.
findings.location: Experiment B — Mechanism paragraph; Scope bullet for `e6_ordershuffle_inputs.jsonl`
scenarios: An executor building the frozen inputs file must choose an algorithm; two executors could produce different permutations, making the committed file non-reproducible without coordination.
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the Mechanism paragraph: 'Permutations are generated deterministically: given the sorted top-level section list, select indices 0, floor(n/3), and floor(2n/3) (no randomness required; n is the section count). For plans with exactly 2 sections, permutation 0 is original order and permutations 1–2 are the swap.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the module where `fleiss_kappa`, `raw_agreement`, and `permute_sections` will live (e.g., `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/e6_metrics.py`) and state the import path the unit test should use.
evidence: Scope says: 'tests/unit/test_e6_agreement.py asserts fleiss_kappa and raw_agreement on hand-computed fixtures … and that permute_sections preserves content'
No `from X import fleiss_kappa, raw_agreement, permute_sections` or module name is given
The harness file is `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/e6_judge_reliability.py` — a non-standard location for importable Python that tests/unit/ would need to reach
findings.finding: The module location (import path) of the pure helpers `fleiss_kappa`, `raw_agreement`, and `permute_sections` is never stated. The unit test `tests/unit/test_e6_agreement.py` must import them from somewhere, and the harness must define or import them — but the Scope section only names the harness file and test file without specifying whether these helpers live in the harness, a shared utility module, or an inline definition.
findings.location: Scope section; AC: 'Pure agreement/permutation helpers are unit-tested'
scenarios: An executor writing test_e6_agreement.py cannot determine the import statement without guessing; if the helpers are defined inline in the harness, importing them from tests/ requires sys.path manipulation that may not be set up.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope section: 'Pure helpers (`fleiss_kappa`, `raw_agreement`, `permute_sections`) are extracted into `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/e6_metrics.py`; `test_e6_agreement.py` imports them as `from harnesses.e6_metrics import ...` with the harness directory on `sys.path` via `conftest.py`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit retry/backoff (e.g. max 3 retries with exponential backoff) for each LLM call in the harness, and define how infra-INDETERMINATE runs are detected and re-queued without appending duplicates to output files.
evidence: 'Infra-INDETERMINATE (LLM tier unavailable, llm_unavailable) is not judge instability — such runs are excluded and re-run so only judge-produced verdicts enter the metric' — no mechanism specified
'E6 does make repeated live, billable LLM calls' — 150 agentic Pass-2 + 45 full-gate = 195+ calls with no retry/backoff spec
ABSENCE: no mention of exponential backoff, max_retries, or circuit-breaker in harness spec
findings.finding: The plan specifies 150 agentic LLM calls + 45 full-gate runs but provides no retry/backoff mechanism for transient LLM failures. The only error-handling language is 'such runs are excluded and re-run' for infra-INDETERMINATE, which is vague — no automatic retry count, backoff strategy, or circuit-breaker is defined. A transient API error mid-run will silently drop votes unless the harness retries, and 'manually re-run the whole experiment' risks re-appending to output files.
findings.location: What section — 'Judge run' (Exp A) and 'Mechanism' (Exp B); LLM-spend honesty section
scenarios: Transient API rate-limit mid-run drops 5 votes; harness exits with N=47 findings × 3 votes; re-run appends duplicates to JSONL, breaking AC proof scripts
LLM tier goes slow; harness hangs silently for hours with no progress log or timeout
findings.suggested_fix: In `e6_judge_reliability.py`, wrap each `pass2_verify` / `finalize_verdict` call in a retry loop (e.g. `tenacity.retry` with exponential backoff, max 3 attempts). Write outputs atomically per-finding (temp file + rename) so a crash mid-run leaves a consistent file. On re-run, skip finding_ids already present in the output file (idempotency guard via a seen-set loaded at startup).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the harness emits per-finding/per-plan progress logs and enforces a per-call timeout (e.g. 120s), surfacing failures rather than hanging silently.
evidence: ABSENCE: no mention of logging, progress reporting, per-call timeout, or alerting in the harness spec
'150 agentic Pass-2 calls … 45 full-gate runs' — a long-running process with no instrumentation spec
findings.finding: With 195+ LLM calls, the plan specifies no progress logging, timeout, or observability instrumentation for the harness run. A silent hang (e.g. one LLM call that never returns) or silent partial failure (e.g. exception swallowed in a loop) would be undetectable until the operator checks the output files manually.
findings.location: What section — Experiment A 'Judge run', Experiment B 'Mechanism'; Scope section ('e6_judge_reliability.py')
scenarios: One `pass2_verify` call hangs on a slow LLM response; harness blocks silently for hours; no metric or log emitted; operator has no visibility
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `timeout=120` parameter to each LLM call wrapper and log `INFO: [A] finding {i}/50 vote {v}/3 complete` after each vote. Log `ERROR: finding {finding_id} vote {v} failed after retries` on exhausted retries so operators can see the failure without inspecting output files.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm `fleiss_kappa` will be hand-rolled using stdlib `math` (consistent with `cohens_kappa` in `eval.py`) rather than pulling `statsmodels`, and document this implementation choice in the Scope section.
evidence: `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py:343` implements `cohens_kappa` using only stdlib `math` — no `statsmodels` or `scipy` import exists anywhere in the codebase (`grep -r 'import statsmodels' .` returns no hits in src/)
Plan says 'pure helpers (`fleiss_kappa`, `raw_agreement`, `permute_sections`)' unit-tested with 'no LLM' but never names the implementation source
Fleiss' κ is a ≤20-line stdlib-math formula (identical pattern to the existing `cohens_kappa`); pulling `statsmodels` would be an unnecessary heavy dependency for this use
findings.finding: The plan designates `fleiss_kappa` as a 'pure helper' to be unit-tested in `tests/unit/test_e6_agreement.py` but never states whether it will be hand-rolled (consistent with the stdlib-only `cohens_kappa` already in `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py:343`) or pulled from `statsmodels.stats.inter_rater` (a large package with zero existing imports in the codebase). If `statsmodels` is introduced as a new dependency for an experiment script, it creates a dependency-surface expansion that violates the project's existing stdlib-only pattern for agreement metrics.
findings.location: Scope section — `tests/unit/test_e6_agreement.py` / 'Pure agreement/permutation helpers' AC
scenarios: An implementer reaches for `statsmodels.stats.inter_rater.fleiss_kappa` as the obvious package, adding a multi-MB transitive dependency to the test environment for a single function call in an experiment harness.
CI breaks or bloats because `statsmodels` (which depends on `scipy`, `numpy`, `pandas`) is not in the locked requirements file and must be added, triggering a dependency audit.
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the Scope or AC: '`fleiss_kappa` is implemented as a ~15-line stdlib-`math` function (no external dependencies), mirroring `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py:cohens_kappa`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify the actual count of `adjudication_corpus.jsonl` rows with `criterion ∈ {G6, E4, T3}` by running `python3 -c "import json; rows=[json.loads(l) for l in open('docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/adjudication_corpus.jsonl')]; cohort=[r for r in rows if r['criterion'] in {'G6','E4','T3'}]; print(len(cohort), 'rows')"` and update the stated count/headroom; if T3 has zero rows, either rebuild the corpus to include T3 findings or revise the cohort definition to G6+E4 only with a corrected row count.
evidence: Multiple searches for 'T3' and '"criterion": "T3"' in docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/adjudication_corpus.jsonl returned zero matches
Search for 'criterion.*T3' also returned zero matches
G6 rows verified present (many hits); E4 rows verified present (many hits); T3 rows: none

Plan quotes: 'rows filtered by criterion ∈ {G6, E4, T3} — exactly the R5 sub-answer's own routing cohort' and 'Verified in the committed file: 74 rows across 66 distinct tickets (advisory 47 / dropped 20 / block 7)'
findings.finding: The plan's core Experiment A sample frame asserts '74 rows across 66 distinct tickets (advisory 47 / dropped 20 / block 7)' filtered by `criterion ∈ {G6, E4, T3}` from `runs/adjudication_corpus.jsonl`. However, the string 'T3' does not appear anywhere in adjudication_corpus.jsonl — not as a primary `criterion` value and not inside any `criteria[]` array. The T3 cohort is entirely absent from the committed corpus, meaning the actual filterable cohort is G6+E4 only. The stated row count (74), ticket count (66), and decision breakdown (advisory 47 / dropped 20 / block 7) are unverified and likely inflated by the false T3 premise. If the true cohort falls below N=50, the plan's stated 24-row headroom disappears and the N=50 deterministic sample may be unachievable.
findings.location: Experiment A — Sample frame (concrete); 'Verified in the committed file: 74 rows across 66 distinct tickets'
scenarios: The harness filters adjudication_corpus.jsonl by criterion ∈ {G6, E4, T3} at runtime and finds only G6+E4 rows; the actual cohort may be ~50-60 rows rather than 74, potentially falling below N=50 after accounting for unsnapshotable tickets
The decision breakdown (advisory 47 / dropped 20 / block 7) cited as 'verified' is wrong, meaning the Fleiss κ base-rate skew correction rationale is built on incorrect prevalence figures
findings.suggested_fix: Run the one-liner above to get the true count. If T3 is absent: (a) update the plan to say 'criterion ∈ {G6, E4}' with the correct row count and headroom, and update the sota:195-199 routing note to reflect that R5's T3 routing is not yet represented in the corpus; OR (b) re-run build_adjudication_corpus.py with explicit T3 inclusion and commit the updated corpus before E6 runs.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how `repo_root` is resolved per ticket in the E6 Exp A harness: either (a) add `repo_root` to `e6_selfconsistency_inputs.jsonl` from the corpus's source ticket metadata, or (b) explicitly state that the agentic path runs non-agentically (tool calls skipped/stubbed) for tickets whose repo_root is unavailable at harness time, and document the coverage gap in the README writeup.
evidence: corpus_sample.json entries include both repo='rebar' (repo_root='/Users/joeoakhart/rebar') and repo='snap' (repo_root='/Users/joeoakhart/snap-oakhart-manual/snap-oakhart-manual')
adjudication_corpus.jsonl stores no repo_root field — only finding_id, ticket_id, criterion, finding text
three_pass.py _verify_agentic calls e2.run_tool(b.name, b.input, repo_root) — repo_root must be correct for the specific ticket's codebase
Plan says 'The N-vote harness reads ONLY this committed file — no live-store dependency at judge time' but does not specify how repo_root is resolved per ticket
findings.finding: Exp A's agentic Pass-2 re-runs require a `repo_root` pointing to the actual repository the ticket lives in, but the frozen input file (`e6_selfconsistency_inputs.jsonl`) stores only plan text — not `repo_root`. The adjudication corpus contains tickets from at least two distinct repos ('rebar' and 'snap', visible in `corpus_sample.json`). The `pass2_verify` agentic path calls `e2.run_tool(b.name, b.input, repo_root)` using the provided repo_root; if a single rebar repo_root is used for all tickets (or none is provided), all `read_file`/`grep` tool calls for snap-repo tickets return empty or wrong results, causing the agentic verifier to answer 'insufficient' on every codebase question uniformly — artificially inflating inter-vote agreement and producing a falsely optimistic self-consistency result.
findings.location: Exp A — Judge run section / 'The N-vote harness reads only this committed file' + three_pass.py pass2_verify / _verify_agentic
scenarios: Snap-repo tickets (T3/G6 findings about snap codebase) are re-judged with rebar repo_root — all file reads return not-found, verifier defaults to insufficient on every binary question, all 3 votes agree (same failure mode), κ appears high
No repo_root is provided at all (agentic=False falls through) — non-agentic path is used for CODEBASE_GROUNDED criteria, defeating the purpose of measuring the agentic R5 path
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `repo_root` field to each row in `e6_selfconsistency_inputs.jsonl` during the snapshot build step (read from the ticket's sidecar or the corpus metadata). In the harness, pass `repo_root=row['repo_root']` to `pass2_verify(..., agentic=True)`. For tickets where the repo is not locally available, either skip the ticket (top up from cohort remainder) or fall back to non-agentic mode and flag the row in the output.

findings.finding: Exp A records `asserted_capability_confirmed` 'where present', but this sub-answer field does not exist in the current `PASS2_TOOL` binary schema in `three_pass.py` (confirmed: `GRADED` list and PASS2_TOOL have no such key). It will be universally absent across all 150 votes, so E6 will not actually measure stability of the R5 sub-answer that is the plan's primary stated motivation for the G6/E4/T3 cohort choice.
findings.location: Experiment A — Judge run section; `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/three_pass.py` lines 110-156 (GRADED, PASS2_TOOL)
findings.suggested_fix: In `e6_judge_reliability.py`, define a local `E6_PASS2_TOOL` that extends the standard binary schema with `asserted_capability_confirmed: {enum: [yes, no, insufficient, na]}` and use this tool for the Exp A agentic calls only (harness-local; no src/rebar change). Update the AC to assert that the sub-answer appears in the recorded votes and include it in the agreement table.

findings.finding: `asserted_capability_confirmed` is referenced as a Pass-2 binary sub-answer to record in Exp A, but its value vocabulary and schema position are never defined in the plan, and the field does not exist in the current `PASS2_TOOL`/`GRADED` schema in `three_pass.py`.
findings.location: Experiment A — Judge run paragraph: 'Record every vote's `decision` and its Pass-2 binary sub-answers (including `asserted_capability_confirmed` where present)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Sub-answer schema note' to Exp A: 'Since R5 has not yet landed, `asserted_capability_confirmed` will be absent from all votes; Exp A records its absence rate as a baseline (0/150 present) — the field's value vocabulary is `yes|no|na` per sota:197 and will be added to PASS2_TOOL in R5. The vote record schema requires: `{finding_id, vote_number, decision, binary: {<GRADED fields>}, asserted_capability_confirmed: null|"yes"|"no"|"na"}`.' This resolves the schema gap without requiring a src/rebar change.

findings.finding: The plan states Exp A will 'Record every vote's `decision` and its Pass-2 binary sub-answers (including `asserted_capability_confirmed` where present)', but `asserted_capability_confirmed` does not exist in the current `PASS2_TOOL` binary schema in `three_pass.py`. The `GRADED` list (lines 110-111) and the `PASS2_TOOL` tool schema (lines 142-156) have no such field. The field will therefore be universally absent across all 150 votes, and E6 will not actually measure the stability of the specific sub-answer that is E6's primary justification for targeting the G6/E4/T3 cohort.
findings.location: Experiment A — 'Judge run' section; three_pass.py GRADED list (lines 110-111) and PASS2_TOOL binary schema (lines 142-156)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a filter in `permute_sections` (or in the Exp B sample selection) to exclude plans where top-level `##` sections contain sequential ordering markers (e.g., section titles that reference prior sections by name, or are labeled with sequential identifiers like E0/E1/E2), and document in the README that the 15-plan permutable subset excludes sequentially-ordered plans.
evidence: corpus_sample.json Tier E story (id: 4387-8fae-9c8b-444b) has ## clusters E0, E1, E2, E3, E4, E5, E6, E7 that are explicitly sequentially ordered ('each in a clean green commit')
corpus_sample.json T3 NDJSON import story (id: e249-1034-3237-44b0) has '## What', '## Scope', '## Acceptance Criteria' with cross-references
sota:44 Wang et al. order-swap finding applies to *pairwise* ranking (where both plans are rated relative to each other) — not to absolute rubric scoring of a single permuted plan
Plan says 'a pure helper permute_sections' with 'content preserved verbatim' but does not filter out plans with sequentially-dependent sections
findings.finding: Exp B's section-permutation mechanism conflates order-sensitivity with semantic-coherence sensitivity for sequentially-ordered plans. Several plans in `corpus_sample.json` (e.g., the Tier E story with clusters E0→E1→E2→E3→…, the T3 NDJSON import plan with two-pass sequencing) have top-level `##` sections whose order is load-bearing: '## E2' references work done in '## E0'. Permuting these sections produces a plan that is genuinely semantically degraded, not merely restated in a different order. A judge that gives this permuted plan a different (worse) verdict is being *correct*, not exhibiting order-sensitivity noise. The experiment would then measure a mix of true order-sensitivity and appropriate semantic-coherence sensitivity, potentially failing the κ floor for the wrong reason and blocking R3 on a spurious signal.
findings.location: Exp B — Mechanism section / 'generate 3 section-order permutations by reordering its top-level ## section blocks'
scenarios: Permuting E0→E2→E4 plan sections: judge correctly identifies E2 section references 'E0 (DONE)' before E0 section appears — gives BLOCK (confused plan), causing a PASS→BLOCK flip that registers as judge instability when it's actually judge correctness
κ falls below 0.6 due to these semantically-broken permutations, triggering a false R3 prerequisite failure
findings.suggested_fix: In the `e6_ordershuffle_inputs.jsonl` build step, after identifying ≥2-section plans, apply a heuristic filter: exclude plans where any `##` heading text contains a reference to another heading text in the same plan (e.g., heading 'E2' when 'E0' also exists as a heading), or where headings follow a clear sequence pattern. Use the remaining plans (which may be fewer than 15) or top up from other corpus sources.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
